claim: SoNash already operates 14 distinct memory mechanisms across 5 tiers, making it one of the most memory-mature Claude Code projects found in the research landscape.

The primary live auto-memory at ~/.claude/projects/.../memory/ has diverged from the canonical-memory copy in .claude/canonical-memory/. The canonical copy is missing ~7 of 18 feedback entries and describes user expertise incorrectly.
The MCP knowledge graph server (mcp__memory) is configured and permitted but shows low active utilization. The checkpoint skill documents it but no evidence of frequent actual usage was found.
The episodic-memory plugin permission is scoped to search only. The full retrieval tool (episodic_memory_show) is not permitted, limiting the plugin to finding but not reading past conversations.
SoNash has 8,473 technical debt items in MASTER_DEBT.jsonl with a documented overwrite hazard — multiple ingestion scripts can regenerate the file, potentially losing deferred/resolved status.
The SoNash learning system has 88.5% effectiveness rate, 444 documented patterns, and 70 automated — an unusually mature behavioral reinforcement system.
STATE_SCHEMA.md documents 10 state files; the actual .claude/state/ directory contains 82 files. The schema document is significantly stale.
claude-mem (thedotmack) is the most widely-adopted Claude Code memory plugin with ~38,400 GitHub stars and a progressive 3-layer disclosure pattern claiming ~10x token savings over naive injection.

everything-claude-code (ECC) is the highest-starred Claude Code plugin found (~124,000 stars), implementing 5 memory layers from session markdown through instincts YAML to a proposed SQLite knowledge graph.

cipher (campfirein) is the only reviewed memory tool implementing reasoning trace capture ('System 2 / Reflection Memory') — storing how the agent thought, not just what happened. Its quality threshold (>=0.4) gates what gets stored.
claude-supermemory requires a $19/month Pro plan for Claude Code plugin access. The free tier does not include it. A Windows stdin bug (Issue #25) was open as of February 2026.

Anthropic's Auto Memory system (GA since v2.1.59) automatically writes session learnings to ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/MEMORY.md, injecting the first 200 lines / 25KB at session start. It is machine-local by design.

Auto Dream (background memory consolidation) exists in the Claude Code codebase as a server-side feature flag (tengu_onyx_plover, enabled:false). The /dream command returns 'Unknown skill: dream' when invoked manually. A community implementation (dream-skill) replicates the 4-phase cycle.

The AGENTS.md standard (Linux Foundation / Agentic AI Foundation) is supported by Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Gemini CLI, Windsurf, Aider, Zed, Warp, RooCode. It addresses the fragmentation problem where CLAUDE.md is ignored by Cursor.
The MCP server ecosystem has 50+ memory-specific servers. PulseMCP lists 413 memory-related MCP entries. The community identified '1,000+ similar projects with zero performance benchmarks.'
Anthropic's official @modelcontextprotocol/server-memory (JSONL knowledge graph) is already configured in SoNash via .mcp.json. It runs natively on Windows with no admin access required.
mem0 reports 26% higher accuracy, 91% faster responses, and 90% token savings on LoCoMo benchmark. AWS chose it as exclusive memory provider for its Agent SDK. These benchmarks are self-reported.
Cursor removed its Memories feature in mid-2025. The community moved to .cursor/rules/ files (structurally identical to CLAUDE.md). No post-mortem explaining why it was removed has been published.

MCP tools are passive (Claude decides when to call them) while hooks are active (fire deterministically at lifecycle events). The supermemory team rebuilt from MCP-first to hooks-only for capture because they could not guarantee capture through MCP alone.
A full memory stack (Auto Memory + CLAUDE.md + MCP server) consumes 10,000-12,000 tokens before any user input. In high-accumulation scenarios this can reach 33,000-54,000 tokens.

The 'check memory at session start' instruction in CLAUDE.md triggers a documented 5-6 minute hang when the MCP server returns empty results (GitHub issue #15140, closed NOT_PLANNED). The correct pattern is hook-based injection.

The three-role model (CLAUDE.md = instructions, Auto Memory = learnings, MCP = knowledge base) emerged independently across multiple research sources as the architectural consensus for organizing Claude Code memory.

The four-file markdown pattern (activeContext.md, productContext.md, progress.md, decisionLog.md) is a de facto community standard across Cline, Roo Code, and Claude Code memory systems — independently converged upon across multiple tools.

Context rot is empirically confirmed: every model tested (Claude Opus 4, GPT-4.1, Gemini 2.5 Pro) degrades with increasing context length. Multiple distractors compound degradation non-linearly. Irrelevant injected memory actively impairs performance.

Auto Memory is explicitly machine-local per Anthropic's official documentation. Setting autoMemoryDirectory to a cloud-synced folder is not officially supported but is not blocked — a potential solo-developer workaround.

GitHub Copilot's citation-backed JIT validation (January 2026) is the most architecturally sophisticated memory approach found. Memories store code line citations; before use, citations are validated against current codebase; stale memories are discarded. It showed 3% precision increase and 7% PR merge rate increase.
Anthropic's engineering blog recommends sub-agent summaries of 1,000-2,000 tokens maximum, file naming that signals purpose, and progressive context discovery over pre-loading.

The compaction loss problem is confirmed across three independent sources: procedural knowledge generated mid-session gets compressed and nuance is lost. Only what was written to memory files before compaction fires is preserved.

MemGPT (UC Berkeley, 2023) established that file-based memory is a recognized architectural pattern (virtual context management), not a workaround. Our file-based approach maps directly to MemGPT's main context / external context model.

The five properties of true episodic memory identify a gap: most systems capture facts but not instance-specific 'when/where/why.' SoNash's MEMORY.md partially satisfies properties 1-3 but fails at property 4 (instance-specific) and partially at 5 (contextual relations).
Reflexion (NeurIPS 2023) demonstrated verbal reinforcement via text files achieves 91% pass@1 on HumanEval vs GPT-4's 80%, validating SoNash's AI_REVIEW_LEARNINGS_LOG.md pattern as research-backed.
A-MAC (March 2026) found that 'content type prior' is the strongest single predictor of whether a memory entry should be stored — stronger than recency, semantic novelty, or utility.

AWS AgentCore's production memory extraction takes 20-40 seconds per memory, confirming that real-time memory extraction during active sessions is not feasible — memory must be built asynchronously post-session.

LongMemEval shows commercial assistants and long-context LLMs exhibit ~30% accuracy drop on memorizing information across sustained interactions. Cross-session memory persistence is unsolved even in production systems.

Only two tools explicitly confirmed Windows compatibility with path remapping for multi-machine scenarios: memoir (5 stars, 27 days old) and mcp-memory-keeper (110 stars). memoir is high-risk; mcp-memory-keeper is the only proven Windows option.
OMEGA Memory requires WSL 2 on Windows and does not run natively, despite claiming #1 LongMemEval benchmark performance (95.4%).

Native Claude Code Auto Memory does not sync cross-locale. Cross-locale sync requires: (A) cloud MCP server (~$19/mo, privacy risk), (B) self-hosted central backend via Railway, or (C) autoMemoryDirectory pointed at cloud-synced folder (unofficial, untested).

git-notes-memory stores memory in git notes that travel with the repository regardless of machine, potentially solving the cross-locale problem. This approach was identified but not deeply researched.
